answers: Quality control (QC) = inspecting products/services to ensure they meet standards. Catch defects before they reach customers.
QA vs QC: QA is proactive (processes to prevent defects), QC is reactive (testing to find defects). Both are essential.
QC methods: inspection, sampling, statistical process control (SPC), Six Sigma, checklists, and testing.
QC tips: define clear standards, train staff, use checklists, sample test batches, and track defect rates over time.
